description_long: This is a large, ancient-style ceramic krater vase with a wide, flared rim and a deep, rounded body. The exterior is predominantly black with intricate reddish-brown painted figures and scenes, likely depicting mythological or ceremonial events in the classical Greek red-figure style. Two small, squared handles are attached to opposite sides just below the rim, blending into the vessel's form. The rim and body show visible signs of age, including surface cracks and minor chips, but the painted decoration remains clear and detailed. The interior appears dark and unadorned, consistent with traditional utilitarian vases. This type of vase would have been used for mixing wine and water during gatherings or rituals.
description: A large, black ceramic krater vase with red-figure painted scenes and squared handles, showing signs of age and wear. The wide rim and deep body are characteristic of ancient Greek ceremonial vessels.
description_view_0: The front view highlights the detailed red-figure scene with reclining and standing figures, as well as the wide, flared rim and one handle partially visible.
description_view_1: This view focuses on one squared handle and shows a partial figure, with the rim's surface cracks more apparent.
description_view_2: The back view displays two standing figures in conversation, with both handles visible on either side of the vase.
description_view_3: This side view centers on the opposite squared handle and a single standing figure, with the rim and body curvature clearly visible.
